authors_text: Anj Simmons,Rajesh Vasa,Antonio Giardina
title: SignalKG: Towards Reasoning about the Underlying Causes of Sensor Observations
title_zh: SignalKG：推理传感器观测的根本原因
comment: 5 pages, 3 figures, to be published in ISWC (Posters/Demos/Industry) 2022, final camera-ready version
comments: 5 pages, 3 figures, to be published in ISWC (Posters/Demos/Industry) 2022, final camera-ready version
abstract: This paper demonstrates our vision for knowledge graphs that assist machines to reason about the cause of signals observed by sensors. We show how the approach allows for constructing smarter surveillance systems that reason about the most likely cause (e.g., an attacker breaking a window) of a signal rather than acting directly on the received signal without consideration for how it was produced.
summary: This paper demonstrates our vision for knowledge graphs that assist machines to reason about the cause of signals observed by sensors. We show how the approach allows for constructing smarter surveillance systems that reason about the most likely cause (e.g., an attacker breaking a window) of a signal rather than acting directly on the received signal without consideration for how it was produced.
abstract_zh: 本文展示了我们对知识图的愿景，该知识图可以帮助机器推理传感器观察到的信号的原因。我们展示了该方法如何允许构建更智能的监视系统，该系统推理信号最可能的原因（例如，攻击者打破窗户），而不是直接对接收到的信号采取行动，而不考虑信号是如何产生的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SignalKG方法，通过知识图谱帮助机器推理传感器信号的潜在原因，提升监控系统的智能性。
AI.motivation: 传统监控系统仅根据传感器信号直接反应，无法分析信号产生的原因，可能导致误判。需通过因果推理提升系统可靠性。
AI.method: 构建结合知识图谱与传感器数据的框架，利用因果推理技术分析信号的潜在原因（如异常事件）。
AI.result: 展示了该方法在监控场景中的有效性，可识别如‘窗户被破坏’等具体原因，而非仅依赖原始信号。
AI.conclusion: 知识图谱驱动的因果推理能显著提升传感器信号分析的准确性，为智能监控系统提供新方向。

authors_text: Xujie Zhang,Yu Sha,Michael C. Kampffmeyer,Zhenyu Xie,Zequn Jie,Chengwen Huang,Jianqing Peng,Xiaodan Liang
title: ARMANI: Part-level Garment-Text Alignment for Unified Cross-Modal Fashion Design
title_zh: ARMANI：统一跨模式时装设计的部分级服装文本对齐
comment: Accepted by ACMMM22
comments: Accepted by ACMMM22
abstract: Cross-modal fashion image synthesis has emerged as one of the most promising directions in the generation domain due to the vast untapped potential of incorporating multiple modalities and the wide range of fashion image applications. To facilitate accurate generation, cross-modal synthesis methods typically rely on Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP) to align textual and garment information. In this work, we argue that simply aligning texture and garment information is not sufficient to capture the semantics of the visual information and therefore propose MaskCLIP. MaskCLIP decomposes the garments into semantic parts, ensuring fine-grained and semantically accurate alignment between the visual and text information. Building on MaskCLIP, we propose ARMANI, a unified cross-modal fashion designer with part-level garment-text alignment. ARMANI discretizes an image into uniform tokens based on a learned cross-modal codebook in its first stage and uses a Transformer to model the distribution of image tokens for a real image given the tokens of the control signals in its second stage. Contrary to prior approaches that also rely on two-stage paradigms, ARMANI introduces textual tokens into the codebook, making it possible for the model to utilize fine-grain semantic information to generate more realistic images. Further, by introducing a cross-modal Transformer, ARMANI is versatile and can accomplish image synthesis from various control signals, such as pure text, sketch images, and partial images. Extensive experiments conducted on our newly collected cross-modal fashion dataset demonstrate that ARMANI generates photo-realistic images in diverse synthesis tasks and outperforms existing state-of-the-art cross-modal image synthesis approaches.Our code is available at https://github.com/Harvey594/ARMANI.
summary: Cross-modal fashion image synthesis has emerged as one of the most promising directions in the generation domain due to the vast untapped potential of incorporating multiple modalities and the wide range of fashion image applications. To facilitate accurate generation, cross-modal synthesis methods typically rely on Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP) to align textual and garment information. In this work, we argue that simply aligning texture and garment information is not sufficient to capture the semantics of the visual information and therefore propose MaskCLIP. MaskCLIP decomposes the garments into semantic parts, ensuring fine-grained and semantically accurate alignment between the visual and text information. Building on MaskCLIP, we propose ARMANI, a unified cross-modal fashion designer with part-level garment-text alignment. ARMANI discretizes an image into uniform tokens based on a learned cross-modal codebook in its first stage and uses a Transformer to model the distribution of image tokens for a real image given the tokens of the control signals in its second stage. Contrary to prior approaches that also rely on two-stage paradigms, ARMANI introduces textual tokens into the codebook, making it possible for the model to utilize fine-grain semantic information to generate more realistic images. Further, by introducing a cross-modal Transformer, ARMANI is versatile and can accomplish image synthesis from various control signals, such as pure text, sketch images, and partial images. Extensive experiments conducted on our newly collected cross-modal fashion dataset demonstrate that ARMANI generates photo-realistic images in diverse synthesis tasks and outperforms existing state-of-the-art cross-modal image synthesis approaches.Our code is available at https://github.com/Harvey594/ARMANI.
abstract_zh: 由于融合多种模态和广泛的时尚图像应用的巨大未开发潜力，跨模态时尚图像合成已成为生成领域最有前途的方向之一。为了促进准确生成，跨模态合成方法通常依赖对比语言图像预训练（CLIP）来对齐文本和服装信息。在这项工作中，我们认为简单地对齐纹理和服装信息不足以捕获视觉信息的语义，因此提出了 MaskCLIP。 MaskCLIP 将服装分解为语义部分，确保视觉和文本信息之间的细粒度和语义准确对齐。在 MaskCLIP 的基础上，我们提出了 ARMANI，一个具有部件级服装文本对齐功能的统一跨模式时装设计师。 ARMANI 在第一阶段根据学习的跨模态密码本将图像离散化为统一标记，并在第二阶段使用 Transformer 对给定控制信号标记的真实图像的图像标记分布进行建模。与同样依赖于两阶段范式的先前方法相反，ARMANI 将文本标记引入到密码本中，使模型能够利用细粒度语义信息来生成更真实的图像。此外，通过引入跨模态 Transformer，ARMANI 具有通用性，可以完成各种控制信号的图像合成，例如纯文本、草图图像和部分图像。对我们新收集的跨模态时尚数据集进行的大量实验表明，ARMANI 在不同的合成任务中生成了逼真的图像，并且优于现有最先进的跨模态图像合成方法。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/Harvey594/ARMANI 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ARMANI框架，通过细粒度服装-文本对齐实现跨模态时尚图像合成。
AI.motivation: 现有方法仅对齐纹理与服装信息，无法准确捕捉视觉语义，导致生成图像不够真实。
AI.method: 提出MaskCLIP分解服装语义部件并构建跨模态码本，ARMANI分两阶段：离散化图像为统一token并引入文本token，使用Transformer建模分布以实现多模态控制信号（文本/草图/部分图像）的图像生成。
AI.result: 在自建跨模态时尚数据集上，ARMANI生成的图像在多样性与真实性指标上优于现有SOTA方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过细粒度语义对齐与跨模态Transformer，ARMANI有效提升了服装图像的生成质量，为统一跨模态时尚设计提供新思路。

authors_text: Tiankai Hang,Huan Yang,Bei Liu,Jianlong Fu,Xin Geng,Baining Guo
title: Language-Guided Face Animation by Recurrent StyleGAN-based Generator
title_zh: 通过基于 StyleGAN 的循环生成器进行语言引导的面部动画
abstract: Recent works on language-guided image manipulation have shown great power of language in providing rich semantics, especially for face images. However, the other natural information, motions, in language is less explored. In this paper, we leverage the motion information and study a novel task, language-guided face animation, that aims to animate a static face image with the help of languages. To better utilize both semantics and motions from languages, we propose a simple yet effective framework. Specifically, we propose a recurrent motion generator to extract a series of semantic and motion information from the language and feed it along with visual information to a pre-trained StyleGAN to generate high-quality frames. To optimize the proposed framework, three carefully designed loss functions are proposed including a regularization loss to keep the face identity, a path length regularization loss to ensure motion smoothness, and a contrastive loss to enable video synthesis with various language guidance in one single model. Extensive experiments with both qualitative and quantitative evaluations on diverse domains (\textit{e.g.,} human face, anime face, and dog face) demonstrate the superiority of our model in generating high-quality and realistic videos from one still image with the guidance of language. Code will be available at https://github.com/TiankaiHang/language-guided-animation.git.
summary: Recent works on language-guided image manipulation have shown great power of language in providing rich semantics, especially for face images. However, the other natural information, motions, in language is less explored. In this paper, we leverage the motion information and study a novel task, language-guided face animation, that aims to animate a static face image with the help of languages. To better utilize both semantics and motions from languages, we propose a simple yet effective framework. Specifically, we propose a recurrent motion generator to extract a series of semantic and motion information from the language and feed it along with visual information to a pre-trained StyleGAN to generate high-quality frames. To optimize the proposed framework, three carefully designed loss functions are proposed including a regularization loss to keep the face identity, a path length regularization loss to ensure motion smoothness, and a contrastive loss to enable video synthesis with various language guidance in one single model. Extensive experiments with both qualitative and quantitative evaluations on diverse domains (\textit{e.g.,} human face, anime face, and dog face) demonstrate the superiority of our model in generating high-quality and realistic videos from one still image with the guidance of language. Code will be available at https://github.com/TiankaiHang/language-guided-animation.git.
abstract_zh: 最近关于语言引导图像处理的工作显示了语言在提供丰富语义方面的强大力量，特别是对于面部图像。然而，语言中的其他自然信息——动作——却很少被探索。在本文中，我们利用运动信息并研究了一项新颖的任务，即语言引导的面部动画，其目的是在语言的帮助下对静态面部图像进行动画处理。为了更好地利用语言的语义和动作，我们提出了一个简单而有效的框架。具体来说，我们提出了一个循环运动生成器，用于从语言中提取一系列语义和运动信息，并将其与视觉信息一起提供给预先训练的 StyleGAN 以生成高质量的帧。为了优化所提出的框架，提出了三个精心设计的损失函数，包括保持面部身份的正则化损失、确保运动平滑的路径长度正则化损失以及在一个模型中实现具有各种语言指导的视频合成的对比损失。对不同领域（\textit{例如，人脸、动漫脸和狗脸）进行定性和定量评估的广泛实验证明了我们的模型在语言指导下从一张静态图像生成高质量和逼真的视频方面的优越性。代码可在 https://github.com/TiankaiHang/language-guided-animation.git 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于语言引导的面部动画生成方法，利用语言中的语义和运动信息，通过改进的StyleGAN生成高质量的动画视频。
AI.motivation: 现有语言引导的图像编辑方法主要关注语义信息，而忽视了语言中的运动信息。本文旨在探索如何利用语言中的运动信息实现静态人脸的动画生成。
AI.method: 设计了一个递归运动生成器，从语言中提取语义和运动信息，并将其与视觉信息结合输入预训练StyleGAN。提出三种损失函数：身份保持正则化损失、路径长度正则化损失和对比损失。
AI.result: 在人脸、动漫脸和狗脸等多领域实验中，模型在生成高质量且逼真的动画视频方面优于现有方法，定性和定量评估均验证了其优越性。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的框架有效结合了语言中的语义与运动信息，实现了单张静态图像在语言指导下生成高质量动画视频，为语言引导的动画生成提供了新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于语言引导的面部动画生成方法，利用语言中的语义和运动信息，通过改进的StyleGAN生成高质量的动画视频。
ai_content.motivation: 现有语言引导的图像编辑方法主要关注语义信息，而忽视了语言中的运动信息。本文旨在探索如何利用语言中的运动信息实现静态人脸的动画生成。
ai_content.method: 设计了一个递归运动生成器，从语言中提取语义和运动信息，并将其与视觉信息结合输入预训练StyleGAN。提出三种损失函数：身份保持正则化损失、路径长度正则化损失和对比损失。
ai_content.result: 在人脸、动漫脸和狗脸等多领域实验中，模型在生成高质量且逼真的动画视频方面优于现有方法，定性和定量评估均验证了其优越性。
ai_content.conclusion: 本文提出的框架有效结合了语言中的语义与运动信息，实现了单张静态图像在语言指导下生成高质量动画视频，为语言引导的动画生成提供了新思路。

search_text: Language-Guided Face Animation by Recurrent StyleGAN-based Generator 通过基于 StyleGAN 的循环生成器进行语言引导的面部动画 Recent works on language-guided image manipulation have shown great power of language in providing rich semantics, especially for face images. However, the other natural information, motions, in language is less explored. In this paper, we leverage the motion information and study a novel task, language-guided face animation, that aims to animate a static face image with the help of languages. To better utilize both semantics and motions from languages, we propose a simple yet effective framework. Specifically, we propose a recurrent motion generator to extract a series of semantic and motion information from the language and feed it along with visual information to a pre-trained StyleGAN to generate high-quality frames. To optimize the proposed framework, three carefully designed loss functions are proposed including a regularization loss to keep the face identity, a path length regularization loss to ensure motion smoothness, and a contrastive loss to enable video synthesis with various language guidance in one single model. Extensive experiments with both qualitative and quantitative evaluations on diverse domains (\textit{e.g.,} human face, anime face, and dog face) demonstrate the superiority of our model in generating high-quality and realistic videos from one still image with the guidance of language. Code will be available at https://github.com/TiankaiHang/language-guided-animation.git. 最近关于语言引导图像处理的工作显示了语言在提供丰富语义方面的强大力量，特别是对于面部图像。然而，语言中的其他自然信息——动作——却很少被探索。在本文中，我们利用运动信息并研究了一项新颖的任务，即语言引导的面部动画，其目的是在语言的帮助下对静态面部图像进行动画处理。为了更好地利用语言的语义和动作，我们提出了一个简单而有效的框架。具体来说，我们提出了一个循环运动生成器，用于从语言中提取一系列语义和运动信息，并将其与视觉信息一起提供给预先训练的 StyleGAN 以生成高质量的帧。为了优化所提出的框架，提出了三个精心设计的损失函数，包括保持面部身份的正则化损失、确保运动平滑的路径长度正则化损失以及在一个模型中实现具有各种语言指导的视频合成的对比损失。对不同领域（\textit{例如，人脸、动漫脸和狗脸）进行定性和定量评估的广泛实验证明了我们的模型在语言指导下从一张静态图像生成高质量和逼真的视频方面的优越性。代码可在 https://github.com/TiankaiHang/language-guided-animation.git 获取。 本文提出了一种基于语言引导的面部动画生成方法，利用语言中的语义和运动信息，通过改进的StyleGAN生成高质量的动画视频。 Tiankai Hang,Huan Yang,Bei Liu,Jianlong Fu,Xin Geng,Baining Guo cs.CV

authors_text: Jia-Xin Zhuang,Xiansong Huang,Yang Yang,Jiancong Chen,Yue Yu,Wei Gao,Ge Li,Jie Chen,Tong Zhang
title: OpenMedIA: Open-Source Medical Image Analysis Toolbox and Benchmark under Heterogeneous AI Computing Platforms
title_zh: OpenMediA：异构AI计算平台下的开源医学图像分析工具箱和基准
comment: 12 pages, 1 figure
comments: 12 pages, 1 figure
abstract: In this paper, we present OpenMedIA, an open-source toolbox library containing a rich set of deep learning methods for medical image analysis under heterogeneous Artificial Intelligence (AI) computing platforms. Various medical image analysis methods, including 2D/3D medical image classification, segmentation, localisation, and detection, have been included in the toolbox with PyTorch and/or MindSpore implementations under heterogeneous NVIDIA and Huawei Ascend computing systems. To our best knowledge, OpenMedIA is the first open-source algorithm library providing compared PyTorch and MindSpore implementations and results on several benchmark datasets. The source codes and models are available at https://git.openi.org.cn/OpenMedIA.
summary: In this paper, we present OpenMedIA, an open-source toolbox library containing a rich set of deep learning methods for medical image analysis under heterogeneous Artificial Intelligence (AI) computing platforms. Various medical image analysis methods, including 2D/3D medical image classification, segmentation, localisation, and detection, have been included in the toolbox with PyTorch and/or MindSpore implementations under heterogeneous NVIDIA and Huawei Ascend computing systems. To our best knowledge, OpenMedIA is the first open-source algorithm library providing compared PyTorch and MindSpore implementations and results on several benchmark datasets. The source codes and models are available at https://git.openi.org.cn/OpenMedIA.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了 OpenMediA，一个开源工具箱库，包含一组丰富的深度学习方法，用于异构人工智能 (AI) 计算平台下的医学图像分析。各种医学图像分析方法，包括 2D/3D 医学图像分类、分割、定位和检测，已包含在异构 NVIDIA 和华为 Ascend 计算系统下通过 PyTorch 和/或 MindSpore 实现的工具箱中。据我们所知，OpenMediA 是第一个开源算法库，提供比较 PyTorch 和 MindSpore 的实现以及在多个基准数据集上的结果。源代码和模型可在https://git.openi.org.cn/OpenMediA获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出OpenMedIA，一个支持异构AI计算平台的开源医学图像分析工具箱，包含多种深度学习方法的PyTorch和MindSpore实现。
AI.motivation: 现有医学图像分析工具缺乏跨异构计算平台（如NVIDIA和华为昇腾）的对比实现，阻碍了算法性能的全面评估与优化。
AI.method: 开发包含2D/3D图像分类、分割、定位和检测的深度学习方法，在NVIDIA和华为昇腾平台下实现PyTorch与MindSpore双框架版本。
AI.result: 首次提供跨异构平台的PyTorch与MindSpore实现对比，开源代码与模型已发布，支持多种基准数据集的测试。
AI.conclusion: OpenMedIA为医学图像分析提供了标准化工具，推动了异构AI计算平台下的算法研究与性能比较。

authors_text: Ibrahim Yilmaz,Mahmoud Abouyoussef
title: FIGO: Enhanced Fingerprint Identification Approach Using GAN and One Shot Learning Techniques
title_zh: FigO：使用 GAN 和一次性学习技术的增强型指纹识别方法
abstract: Fingerprint evidence plays an important role in a criminal investigation for the identification of individuals. Although various techniques have been proposed for fingerprint classification and feature extraction, automated fingerprint identification of fingerprints is still in its earliest stage. The performance of traditional \textit{Automatic Fingerprint Identification System} (AFIS) depends on the presence of valid minutiae points and still requires human expert assistance in feature extraction and identification stages. Based on this motivation, we propose a Fingerprint Identification approach based on Generative adversarial network and One-shot learning techniques (FIGO). Our solution contains two components: fingerprint enhancement tier and fingerprint identification tier. First, we propose a Pix2Pix model to transform low-quality fingerprint images to a higher level of fingerprint images pixel by pixel directly in the fingerprint enhancement tier. With the proposed enhancement algorithm, the fingerprint identification model's performance is significantly improved. Furthermore, we develop another existing solution based on Gabor filters as a benchmark to compare with the proposed model by observing the fingerprint device's recognition accuracy. Experimental results show that our proposed Pix2pix model has better support than the baseline approach for fingerprint identification. Second, we construct a fully automated fingerprint feature extraction model using a one-shot learning approach to differentiate each fingerprint from the others in the fingerprint identification process. Two twin convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with shared weights and parameters are used to obtain the feature vectors in this process. Using the proposed method, we demonstrate that it is possible to learn necessary information from only one training sample with high accuracy.
summary: Fingerprint evidence plays an important role in a criminal investigation for the identification of individuals. Although various techniques have been proposed for fingerprint classification and feature extraction, automated fingerprint identification of fingerprints is still in its earliest stage. The performance of traditional \textit{Automatic Fingerprint Identification System} (AFIS) depends on the presence of valid minutiae points and still requires human expert assistance in feature extraction and identification stages. Based on this motivation, we propose a Fingerprint Identification approach based on Generative adversarial network and One-shot learning techniques (FIGO). Our solution contains two components: fingerprint enhancement tier and fingerprint identification tier. First, we propose a Pix2Pix model to transform low-quality fingerprint images to a higher level of fingerprint images pixel by pixel directly in the fingerprint enhancement tier. With the proposed enhancement algorithm, the fingerprint identification model's performance is significantly improved. Furthermore, we develop another existing solution based on Gabor filters as a benchmark to compare with the proposed model by observing the fingerprint device's recognition accuracy. Experimental results show that our proposed Pix2pix model has better support than the baseline approach for fingerprint identification. Second, we construct a fully automated fingerprint feature extraction model using a one-shot learning approach to differentiate each fingerprint from the others in the fingerprint identification process. Two twin convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with shared weights and parameters are used to obtain the feature vectors in this process. Using the proposed method, we demonstrate that it is possible to learn necessary information from only one training sample with high accuracy.
abstract_zh: 指纹证据在刑事调查中识别个人身份方面发挥着重要作用。尽管已经提出了各种用于指纹分类和特征提取的技术，但指纹的自动指纹识别仍处于最早阶段。传统\textit{自动指纹识别系统}（AFIS）的性能取决于有效细节点的存在，并且在特征提取和识别阶段仍然需要人类专家的协助。基于这一动机，我们提出了一种基于生成对抗网络和一次性学习技术（FIGO）的指纹识别方法。我们的解决方案包含两个组件：指纹增强层和指纹识别层。首先，我们提出了一种 Pix2Pix 模型，可以直接在指纹增强层中将低质量指纹图像逐像素转换为更高级别的指纹图像。通过所提出的增强算法，指纹识别模型的性能得到显着提高。此外，我们开发了另一种基于 Gabor 滤波器作为基准的现有解决方案，通过观察指纹设备的识别精度来与所提出的模型进行比较。实验结果表明，我们提出的 Pix2pix 模型比基线方法对指纹识别具有更好的支持。其次，我们使用一次性学习方法构建了一个全自动指纹特征提取模型，以在指纹识别过程中区分每个指纹与其他指纹。在此过程中，使用两个具有共享权重和参数的双卷积神经网络（CNN）来获取特征向量。使用所提出的方法，我们证明可以仅从一个训练样本中高精度地学习必要的信息。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合生成对抗网络（GAN）和单次学习技术的指纹识别方法（FIGO），旨在提升指纹识别的自动化水平和准确性。
AI.motivation: 传统自动指纹识别系统（AFIS）依赖于有效的细节点提取且需人工辅助，存在效率低、依赖性强的问题，亟需更高效自动化的解决方案。
AI.method: FIGO包含两个模块：1）使用Pix2Pix模型逐像素增强低质量指纹图像；2）通过双卷积神经网络（共享权重）实现单次学习的指纹特征提取与区分。
AI.result: 实验表明，Pix2Pix模型在指纹识别准确率上优于基准的Gabor滤波方法，且单次学习模型在仅使用单个样本时仍能保持高识别精度。
AI.conclusion: FIGO通过图像增强与单次学习技术显著提升了指纹识别的自动化水平，为实际刑侦应用提供了更高效可靠的解决方案。

search_text: FIGO: Enhanced Fingerprint Identification Approach Using GAN and One Shot Learning Techniques FigO：使用 GAN 和一次性学习技术的增强型指纹识别方法 Fingerprint evidence plays an important role in a criminal investigation for the identification of individuals. Although various techniques have been proposed for fingerprint classification and feature extraction, automated fingerprint identification of fingerprints is still in its earliest stage. The performance of traditional \textit{Automatic Fingerprint Identification System} (AFIS) depends on the presence of valid minutiae points and still requires human expert assistance in feature extraction and identification stages. Based on this motivation, we propose a Fingerprint Identification approach based on Generative adversarial network and One-shot learning techniques (FIGO). Our solution contains two components: fingerprint enhancement tier and fingerprint identification tier. First, we propose a Pix2Pix model to transform low-quality fingerprint images to a higher level of fingerprint images pixel by pixel directly in the fingerprint enhancement tier. With the proposed enhancement algorithm, the fingerprint identification model's performance is significantly improved. Furthermore, we develop another existing solution based on Gabor filters as a benchmark to compare with the proposed model by observing the fingerprint device's recognition accuracy. Experimental results show that our proposed Pix2pix model has better support than the baseline approach for fingerprint identification. Second, we construct a fully automated fingerprint feature extraction model using a one-shot learning approach to differentiate each fingerprint from the others in the fingerprint identification process. Two twin convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with shared weights and parameters are used to obtain the feature vectors in this process. Using the proposed method, we demonstrate that it is possible to learn necessary information from only one training sample with high accuracy. 指纹证据在刑事调查中识别个人身份方面发挥着重要作用。尽管已经提出了各种用于指纹分类和特征提取的技术，但指纹的自动指纹识别仍处于最早阶段。传统\textit{自动指纹识别系统}（AFIS）的性能取决于有效细节点的存在，并且在特征提取和识别阶段仍然需要人类专家的协助。基于这一动机，我们提出了一种基于生成对抗网络和一次性学习技术（FIGO）的指纹识别方法。我们的解决方案包含两个组件：指纹增强层和指纹识别层。首先，我们提出了一种 Pix2Pix 模型，可以直接在指纹增强层中将低质量指纹图像逐像素转换为更高级别的指纹图像。通过所提出的增强算法，指纹识别模型的性能得到显着提高。此外，我们开发了另一种基于 Gabor 滤波器作为基准的现有解决方案，通过观察指纹设备的识别精度来与所提出的模型进行比较。实验结果表明，我们提出的 Pix2pix 模型比基线方法对指纹识别具有更好的支持。其次，我们使用一次性学习方法构建了一个全自动指纹特征提取模型，以在指纹识别过程中区分每个指纹与其他指纹。在此过程中，使用两个具有共享权重和参数的双卷积神经网络（CNN）来获取特征向量。使用所提出的方法，我们证明可以仅从一个训练样本中高精度地学习必要的信息。 本文提出一种结合生成对抗网络（GAN）和单次学习技术的指纹识别方法（FIGO），旨在提升指纹识别的自动化水平和准确性。 Ibrahim Yilmaz,Mahmoud Abouyoussef cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Zhong Ji,Zhishen Hou,Xiyao Liu,Yanwei Pang,Xuelong Li
title: Memorizing Complementation Network for Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning
title_zh: 记住小样本类增量学习的补充网络
abstract: Few-shot Class-Incremental Learning (FSCIL) aims at learning new concepts continually with only a few samples, which is prone to suffer the catastrophic forgetting and overfitting problems. The inaccessibility of old classes and the scarcity of the novel samples make it formidable to realize the trade-off between retaining old knowledge and learning novel concepts. Inspired by that different models memorize different knowledge when learning novel concepts, we propose a Memorizing Complementation Network (MCNet) to ensemble multiple models that complements the different memorized knowledge with each other in novel tasks. Additionally, to update the model with few novel samples, we develop a Prototype Smoothing Hard-mining Triplet (PSHT) loss to push the novel samples away from not only each other in current task but also the old distribution. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets, e.g., CIFAR100, miniImageNet and CUB200, have demonstrated the superiority of our proposed method.
summary: Few-shot Class-Incremental Learning (FSCIL) aims at learning new concepts continually with only a few samples, which is prone to suffer the catastrophic forgetting and overfitting problems. The inaccessibility of old classes and the scarcity of the novel samples make it formidable to realize the trade-off between retaining old knowledge and learning novel concepts. Inspired by that different models memorize different knowledge when learning novel concepts, we propose a Memorizing Complementation Network (MCNet) to ensemble multiple models that complements the different memorized knowledge with each other in novel tasks. Additionally, to update the model with few novel samples, we develop a Prototype Smoothing Hard-mining Triplet (PSHT) loss to push the novel samples away from not only each other in current task but also the old distribution. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets, e.g., CIFAR100, miniImageNet and CUB200, have demonstrated the superiority of our proposed method.
abstract_zh: 少样本类增量学习（FSCIL）旨在仅用少量样本不断学习新概念，这很容易遭受灾难性遗忘和过度拟合问题。旧课程的难以接近和新样本的稀缺使得很难实现保留旧知识和学习新概念之间的权衡。受不同模型在学习新概念时记忆不同知识的启发，我们提出了一种记忆互补网络（MCNet）来集成多个模型，在新任务中相互补充不同记忆的知识。此外，为了用少量新样本更新模型，我们开发了原型平滑硬挖掘三元组（PSHT）损失，以将新样本不仅在当前任务中相互推开，而且在旧分布中推开。对三个基准数据集（例如 CIFAR100、miniImageNet 和 CUB200）的大量实验证明了我们提出的方法的优越性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种记忆互补网络（MCNet），用于解决少样本类别增量学习（FSCIL）中的灾难性遗忘和过拟合问题。
AI.motivation: 在FSCIL任务中，旧类别数据不可访问且新类别样本稀缺，导致难以平衡保留旧知识与学习新概念的矛盾。
AI.method: 通过构建多个互补记忆模型（MCNet）实现知识互补，并设计原型平滑硬挖掘三元组（PSHT）损失函数，推动新样本远离当前任务样本及旧类别分布。
AI.result: 在CIFAR100、miniImageNet和CUB200三个基准数据集上的实验验证了该方法的优越性。
AI.conclusion: MCNet通过多模型知识互补与PSHT损失函数有效缓解了FSCIL中的灾难性遗忘问题，取得了优于现有方法的性能。

authors_text: Sangjun Han,Hyeongrae Ihm,Moontae Lee,Woohyung Lim
title: Symbolic Music Loop Generation with Neural Discrete Representations
title_zh: 使用神经离散表示的符号音乐循环生成
comment: Accepted at ISMIR 2022
comments: Accepted at ISMIR 2022
abstract: Since most of music has repetitive structures from motifs to phrases, repeating musical ideas can be a basic operation for music composition. The basic block that we focus on is conceptualized as loops which are essential ingredients of music. Furthermore, meaningful note patterns can be formed in a finite space, so it is sufficient to represent them with combinations of discrete symbols as done in other domains. In this work, we propose symbolic music loop generation via learning discrete representations. We first extract loops from MIDI datasets using a loop detector and then learn an autoregressive model trained by discrete latent codes of the extracted loops. We show that our model outperforms well-known music generative models in terms of both fidelity and diversity, evaluating on random space. Our code and supplementary materials are available at https://github.com/sjhan91/Loop_VQVAE_Official.
summary: Since most of music has repetitive structures from motifs to phrases, repeating musical ideas can be a basic operation for music composition. The basic block that we focus on is conceptualized as loops which are essential ingredients of music. Furthermore, meaningful note patterns can be formed in a finite space, so it is sufficient to represent them with combinations of discrete symbols as done in other domains. In this work, we propose symbolic music loop generation via learning discrete representations. We first extract loops from MIDI datasets using a loop detector and then learn an autoregressive model trained by discrete latent codes of the extracted loops. We show that our model outperforms well-known music generative models in terms of both fidelity and diversity, evaluating on random space. Our code and supplementary materials are available at https://github.com/sjhan91/Loop_VQVAE_Official.
abstract_zh: 由于大多数音乐都具有从主题到乐句的重复结构，因此重复音乐思想可以成为音乐创作的基本操作。我们关注的基本块被概念化为循环，它是音乐的基本成分。此外，有意义的音符模式可以在有限的空间中形成，因此用离散符号的组合来表示它们就足够了，就像在其他领域中所做的那样。在这项工作中，我们提出通过学习离散表示来生成符号音乐循环。我们首先使用循环检测器从 MIDI 数据集中提取循环，然后学习由提取循环的离散潜在代码训练的自回归模型。我们证明，我们的模型在保真度和多样性方面都优于著名的音乐生成模型，并对随机空间进行评估。我们的代码和补充材料可在 https://github.com/sjhan91/Loop_VQVAE_Official 获取。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于神经离散表示的符号音乐循环生成方法
AI.motivation: 音乐中存在大量重复结构，循环作为基本构建块，可用离散符号有效表示，从而提升生成效率与质量
AI.method: 使用循环检测器从MIDI数据中提取循环，训练基于离散潜在代码的自回归模型进行生成
AI.result: 模型在保真度和多样性指标上优于现有音乐生成模型
AI.conclusion: 验证了离散表示在音乐生成中的有效性，为符号音乐创作提供新方法

authors_text: Cristina-Madalina Dragan,Muhammad Muneeb Saad,Mubashir Husain Rehmani,Ruairi O'Reilly
title: Evaluating the Quality and Diversity of DCGAN-based Generatively Synthesized Diabetic Retinopathy Imagery
title_zh: 评估基于 DCGAN 的生成合成糖尿病视网膜病变图像的质量和多样性
comment: 29 Pages, 8 Figures, submitted to MEDAL23: Advances in Deep Generative Models for Medical Artificial Intelligence (Springer Nature series)
comments: 29 Pages, 8 Figures, submitted to MEDAL23: Advances in Deep Generative Models for Medical Artificial Intelligence (Springer Nature series)
abstract: Publicly available diabetic retinopathy (DR) datasets are imbalanced, containing limited numbers of images with DR. This imbalance contributes to overfitting when training machine learning classifiers. The impact of this imbalance is exacerbated as the severity of the DR stage increases, affecting the classifiers' diagnostic capacity. The imbalance can be addressed using Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to augment the datasets with synthetic images. Generating synthetic images is advantageous if high-quality and diversified images are produced. To evaluate the quality and diversity of synthetic images, several evaluation metrics, such as Multi-Scale Structural Similarity Index (MS-SSIM), Cosine Distance (CD), and Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) are used. Understanding the effectiveness of each metric in evaluating the quality and diversity of GAN-based synthetic images is critical to select images for augmentation. To date, there has been limited analysis of the appropriateness of these metrics in the context of biomedical imagery. This work contributes an empirical assessment of these evaluation metrics as applied to synthetic Proliferative DR imagery generated by a Deep Convolutional GAN (DCGAN). Furthermore, the metrics' capacity to indicate the quality and diversity of synthetic images and a correlation with classifier performance is undertaken. This enables a quantitative selection of synthetic imagery and an informed augmentation strategy. Results indicate that FID is suitable for evaluating the quality, while MS-SSIM and CD are suitable for evaluating the diversity of synthetic imagery. Furthermore, the superior performance of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and EfficientNet classifiers, as indicated by the F1 and AUC scores, for the augmented datasets demonstrates the efficacy of synthetic imagery to augment the imbalanced dataset.
summary: Publicly available diabetic retinopathy (DR) datasets are imbalanced, containing limited numbers of images with DR. This imbalance contributes to overfitting when training machine learning classifiers. The impact of this imbalance is exacerbated as the severity of the DR stage increases, affecting the classifiers' diagnostic capacity. The imbalance can be addressed using Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to augment the datasets with synthetic images. Generating synthetic images is advantageous if high-quality and diversified images are produced. To evaluate the quality and diversity of synthetic images, several evaluation metrics, such as Multi-Scale Structural Similarity Index (MS-SSIM), Cosine Distance (CD), and Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) are used. Understanding the effectiveness of each metric in evaluating the quality and diversity of GAN-based synthetic images is critical to select images for augmentation. To date, there has been limited analysis of the appropriateness of these metrics in the context of biomedical imagery. This work contributes an empirical assessment of these evaluation metrics as applied to synthetic Proliferative DR imagery generated by a Deep Convolutional GAN (DCGAN). Furthermore, the metrics' capacity to indicate the quality and diversity of synthetic images and a correlation with classifier performance is undertaken. This enables a quantitative selection of synthetic imagery and an informed augmentation strategy. Results indicate that FID is suitable for evaluating the quality, while MS-SSIM and CD are suitable for evaluating the diversity of synthetic imagery. Furthermore, the superior performance of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and EfficientNet classifiers, as indicated by the F1 and AUC scores, for the augmented datasets demonstrates the efficacy of synthetic imagery to augment the imbalanced dataset.
abstract_zh: 公开的糖尿病视网膜病变 (DR) 数据集不平衡，包含的 DR 图像数量有限。这种不平衡会导致训练机器学习分类器时过度拟合。随着 DR 阶段严重性的增加，这种不平衡的影响会加剧，从而影响分类器的诊断能力。可以使用生成对抗网络（GAN）通过合成图像来增强数据集来解决这种不平衡问题。如果生成高质量和多样化的图像，生成合成图像是有利的。为了评估合成图像的质量和多样性，使用了多种评估指标，例如多尺度结构相似性指数（MS-SSIM）、余弦距离（CD）和Fréchet起始距离（FID）。了解每个指标在评估基于 GAN 的合成图像的质量和多样性方面的有效性对于选择用于增强的图像至关重要。迄今为止，对这些指标在生物医学图像背景下的适当性的分析还很有限。这项工作对这些评估指标进行了实证评估，并将其应用于由深度卷积 GAN (DCGAN) 生成的合成增殖 DR 图像。此外，还采用了指标来指示合成图像的质量和多样性以及与分类器性能的相关性。这使得合成图像的定量选择和知情的增强策略成为可能。结果表明，FID适合评估合成图像的质量，而MS-SSIM和CD适合评估合成图像的多样性。此外，如 F1 和 AUC 分数所示，卷积神经网络 (CNN) 和 EfficientNet 分类器对于增强数据集的卓越性能证明了合成图像增强不平衡数据集的功效。

AI.tldr: 本文评估了基于DCGAN生成的糖尿病视网膜病变合成图像的质量和多样性，通过多种指标分析其有效性。
AI.motivation: 公开的糖尿病视网膜病变数据集存在类别不平衡问题，导致分类模型过拟合，尤其在严重病变阶段影响诊断效果，需通过生成合成图像进行数据增强。
AI.method: 采用DCGAN生成合成图像，并使用MS-SSIM、余弦距离（CD）和Fréchet inception距离（FID）等指标评估图像质量与多样性，结合CNN和EfficientNet分类器验证增强效果。
AI.result: FID适合评估图像质量，MS-SSIM和CD适合评估多样性；使用合成图像增强后，分类器的F1和AUC指标显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 实验验证了不同指标在评估合成医学图像中的适用性，并证明合成图像能有效改善不平衡数据集的分类性能，为数据增强策略提供量化依据。

search_text: Evaluating the Quality and Diversity of DCGAN-based Generatively Synthesized Diabetic Retinopathy Imagery 评估基于 DCGAN 的生成合成糖尿病视网膜病变图像的质量和多样性 Publicly available diabetic retinopathy (DR) datasets are imbalanced, containing limited numbers of images with DR. This imbalance contributes to overfitting when training machine learning classifiers. The impact of this imbalance is exacerbated as the severity of the DR stage increases, affecting the classifiers' diagnostic capacity. The imbalance can be addressed using Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to augment the datasets with synthetic images. Generating synthetic images is advantageous if high-quality and diversified images are produced. To evaluate the quality and diversity of synthetic images, several evaluation metrics, such as Multi-Scale Structural Similarity Index (MS-SSIM), Cosine Distance (CD), and Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) are used. Understanding the effectiveness of each metric in evaluating the quality and diversity of GAN-based synthetic images is critical to select images for augmentation. To date, there has been limited analysis of the appropriateness of these metrics in the context of biomedical imagery. This work contributes an empirical assessment of these evaluation metrics as applied to synthetic Proliferative DR imagery generated by a Deep Convolutional GAN (DCGAN). Furthermore, the metrics' capacity to indicate the quality and diversity of synthetic images and a correlation with classifier performance is undertaken. This enables a quantitative selection of synthetic imagery and an informed augmentation strategy. Results indicate that FID is suitable for evaluating the quality, while MS-SSIM and CD are suitable for evaluating the diversity of synthetic imagery. Furthermore, the superior performance of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and EfficientNet classifiers, as indicated by the F1 and AUC scores, for the augmented datasets demonstrates the efficacy of synthetic imagery to augment the imbalanced dataset. 公开的糖尿病视网膜病变 (DR) 数据集不平衡，包含的 DR 图像数量有限。这种不平衡会导致训练机器学习分类器时过度拟合。随着 DR 阶段严重性的增加，这种不平衡的影响会加剧，从而影响分类器的诊断能力。可以使用生成对抗网络（GAN）通过合成图像来增强数据集来解决这种不平衡问题。如果生成高质量和多样化的图像，生成合成图像是有利的。为了评估合成图像的质量和多样性，使用了多种评估指标，例如多尺度结构相似性指数（MS-SSIM）、余弦距离（CD）和Fréchet起始距离（FID）。了解每个指标在评估基于 GAN 的合成图像的质量和多样性方面的有效性对于选择用于增强的图像至关重要。迄今为止，对这些指标在生物医学图像背景下的适当性的分析还很有限。这项工作对这些评估指标进行了实证评估，并将其应用于由深度卷积 GAN (DCGAN) 生成的合成增殖 DR 图像。此外，还采用了指标来指示合成图像的质量和多样性以及与分类器性能的相关性。这使得合成图像的定量选择和知情的增强策略成为可能。结果表明，FID适合评估合成图像的质量，而MS-SSIM和CD适合评估合成图像的多样性。此外，如 F1 和 AUC 分数所示，卷积神经网络 (CNN) 和 EfficientNet 分类器对于增强数据集的卓越性能证明了合成图像增强不平衡数据集的功效。 本文评估了基于DCGAN生成的糖尿病视网膜病变合成图像的质量和多样性，通过多种指标分析其有效性。 Cristina-Madalina Dragan,Muhammad Muneeb Saad,Mubashir Husain Rehmani,Ruairi O'Reilly eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Gabriel Ilharco,Mitchell Wortsman,Samir Yitzhak Gadre,Shuran Song,Hannaneh Hajishirzi,Simon Kornblith,Ali Farhadi,Ludwig Schmidt
title: Patching open-vocabulary models by interpolating weights
title_zh: 通过插值权重来修补开放词汇模型
comment: 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)
comments: 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)
abstract: Open-vocabulary models like CLIP achieve high accuracy across many image classification tasks. However, there are still settings where their zero-shot performance is far from optimal. We study model patching, where the goal is to improve accuracy on specific tasks without degrading accuracy on tasks where performance is already adequate. Towards this goal, we introduce PAINT, a patching method that uses interpolations between the weights of a model before fine-tuning and the weights after fine-tuning on a task to be patched. On nine tasks where zero-shot CLIP performs poorly, PAINT increases accuracy by 15 to 60 percentage points while preserving accuracy on ImageNet within one percentage point of the zero-shot model. PAINT also allows a single model to be patched on multiple tasks and improves with model scale. Furthermore, we identify cases of broad transfer, where patching on one task increases accuracy on other tasks even when the tasks have disjoint classes. Finally, we investigate applications beyond common benchmarks such as counting or reducing the impact of typographic attacks on CLIP. Our findings demonstrate that it is possible to expand the set of tasks on which open-vocabulary models achieve high accuracy without re-training them from scratch.
summary: Open-vocabulary models like CLIP achieve high accuracy across many image classification tasks. However, there are still settings where their zero-shot performance is far from optimal. We study model patching, where the goal is to improve accuracy on specific tasks without degrading accuracy on tasks where performance is already adequate. Towards this goal, we introduce PAINT, a patching method that uses interpolations between the weights of a model before fine-tuning and the weights after fine-tuning on a task to be patched. On nine tasks where zero-shot CLIP performs poorly, PAINT increases accuracy by 15 to 60 percentage points while preserving accuracy on ImageNet within one percentage point of the zero-shot model. PAINT also allows a single model to be patched on multiple tasks and improves with model scale. Furthermore, we identify cases of broad transfer, where patching on one task increases accuracy on other tasks even when the tasks have disjoint classes. Finally, we investigate applications beyond common benchmarks such as counting or reducing the impact of typographic attacks on CLIP. Our findings demonstrate that it is possible to expand the set of tasks on which open-vocabulary models achieve high accuracy without re-training them from scratch.
abstract_zh: 像 CLIP 这样的开放词汇模型可以在许多图像分类任务中实现高精度。然而，在某些设置中，它们的零样本性能仍然远非最佳。我们研究模型修补，其目标是提高特定任务的准确性，而不降低性能已经足够的任务的准确性。为了实现这一目标，我们引入了 PAINT，这是一种修补方法，它在要修补的任务上使用微调之前的模型权重和微调之后的权重之间的插值。在零样本 CLIP 表现不佳的 9 个任务中，PAINT 将准确率提高了 15 到 60 个百分点，同时将 ImageNet 上的准确率保持在零样本模型 1 个百分点以内。 PAINT 还允许在多个任务上修补单个模型，并随着模型规模进行改进。此外，我们还确定了广泛迁移的情况，其中对一项任务进行修补可以提高其他任务的准确性，即使这些任务具有不相交的类。最后，我们研究了常见基准之外的应用程序，例如计算或减少印刷攻击对 CLIP 的影响。我们的研究结果表明，可以扩展开放词汇模型实现高精度的任务集，而无需从头开始重新训练它们。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PAINT方法，通过插值微调前后模型权重，提升开放词汇模型在特定任务的准确性而不影响原有性能。
AI.motivation: 开放词汇模型（如CLIP）在零样本场景下存在性能不足，需在不重新训练模型的前提下针对性优化特定任务表现。
AI.method: PAINT方法利用微调前与微调后模型权重的插值，结合任务需求调整模型参数，同时保持其他任务性能稳定。
AI.result: 在9个任务上准确率提升15-60个百分点，ImageNet性能下降不超过1%，支持多任务修补及跨任务迁移，适用于计数和抗字体攻击等场景。
AI.conclusion: 无需从头训练即可扩展开放词汇模型的任务覆盖范围，验证了权重插值在模型优化中的有效性。

search_text: Patching open-vocabulary models by interpolating weights 通过插值权重来修补开放词汇模型 Open-vocabulary models like CLIP achieve high accuracy across many image classification tasks. However, there are still settings where their zero-shot performance is far from optimal. We study model patching, where the goal is to improve accuracy on specific tasks without degrading accuracy on tasks where performance is already adequate. Towards this goal, we introduce PAINT, a patching method that uses interpolations between the weights of a model before fine-tuning and the weights after fine-tuning on a task to be patched. On nine tasks where zero-shot CLIP performs poorly, PAINT increases accuracy by 15 to 60 percentage points while preserving accuracy on ImageNet within one percentage point of the zero-shot model. PAINT also allows a single model to be patched on multiple tasks and improves with model scale. Furthermore, we identify cases of broad transfer, where patching on one task increases accuracy on other tasks even when the tasks have disjoint classes. Finally, we investigate applications beyond common benchmarks such as counting or reducing the impact of typographic attacks on CLIP. Our findings demonstrate that it is possible to expand the set of tasks on which open-vocabulary models achieve high accuracy without re-training them from scratch. 像 CLIP 这样的开放词汇模型可以在许多图像分类任务中实现高精度。然而，在某些设置中，它们的零样本性能仍然远非最佳。我们研究模型修补，其目标是提高特定任务的准确性，而不降低性能已经足够的任务的准确性。为了实现这一目标，我们引入了 PAINT，这是一种修补方法，它在要修补的任务上使用微调之前的模型权重和微调之后的权重之间的插值。在零样本 CLIP 表现不佳的 9 个任务中，PAINT 将准确率提高了 15 到 60 个百分点，同时将 ImageNet 上的准确率保持在零样本模型 1 个百分点以内。 PAINT 还允许在多个任务上修补单个模型，并随着模型规模进行改进。此外，我们还确定了广泛迁移的情况，其中对一项任务进行修补可以提高其他任务的准确性，即使这些任务具有不相交的类。最后，我们研究了常见基准之外的应用程序，例如计算或减少印刷攻击对 CLIP 的影响。我们的研究结果表明，可以扩展开放词汇模型实现高精度的任务集，而无需从头开始重新训练它们。 本文提出PAINT方法，通过插值微调前后模型权重，提升开放词汇模型在特定任务的准确性而不影响原有性能。 Gabriel Ilharco,Mitchell Wortsman,Samir Yitzhak Gadre,Shuran Song,Hannaneh Hajishirzi,Simon Kornblith,Ali Farhadi,Ludwig Schmidt cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Anil Yaman,Joel Z. Leibo,Giovanni Iacca,Sang Wan Lee
title: The emergence of division of labor through decentralized social sanctioning
title_zh: 通过分散的社会制裁出现劳动分工
abstract: Human ecological success relies on our characteristic ability to flexibly self-organize into cooperative social groups, the most successful of which employ substantial specialization and division of labor. Unlike most other animals, humans learn by trial and error during their lives what role to take on. However, when some critical roles are more attractive than others, and individuals are self-interested, then there is a social dilemma: each individual would prefer others take on the critical but unremunerative roles so they may remain free to take one that pays better. But disaster occurs if all act thusly and a critical role goes unfilled. In such situations learning an optimum role distribution may not be possible. Consequently, a fundamental question is: how can division of labor emerge in groups of self-interested lifetime-learning individuals? Here we show that by introducing a model of social norms, which we regard as emergent patterns of decentralized social sanctioning, it becomes possible for groups of self-interested individuals to learn a productive division of labor involving all critical roles. Such social norms work by redistributing rewards within the population to disincentivize antisocial roles while incentivizing prosocial roles that do not intrinsically pay as well as others.
summary: Human ecological success relies on our characteristic ability to flexibly self-organize into cooperative social groups, the most successful of which employ substantial specialization and division of labor. Unlike most other animals, humans learn by trial and error during their lives what role to take on. However, when some critical roles are more attractive than others, and individuals are self-interested, then there is a social dilemma: each individual would prefer others take on the critical but unremunerative roles so they may remain free to take one that pays better. But disaster occurs if all act thusly and a critical role goes unfilled. In such situations learning an optimum role distribution may not be possible. Consequently, a fundamental question is: how can division of labor emerge in groups of self-interested lifetime-learning individuals? Here we show that by introducing a model of social norms, which we regard as emergent patterns of decentralized social sanctioning, it becomes possible for groups of self-interested individuals to learn a productive division of labor involving all critical roles. Such social norms work by redistributing rewards within the population to disincentivize antisocial roles while incentivizing prosocial roles that do not intrinsically pay as well as others.
abstract_zh: 人类生态的成功依赖于我们灵活地自组织成合作性社会群体的特有能力，其中最成功的群体采用了大量的专业化和劳动分工。与大多数其他动物不同，人类在一生中通过尝试和错误来学习扮演什么角色。然而，当某些关键角色比其他角色更有吸引力，并且个人是自私的，那么就会出现一种社会困境：每个人都更喜欢其他人担任关键但无报酬的角色，这样他们就可以自由地担任报酬更高的角色。但如果所有人都这样做并且关键角色没有得到填补，灾难就会发生。在这种情况下，学习最佳的角色分配可能是不可能的。因此，一个基本问题是：如何在自私的终身学习个体群体中出现分工？在这里，我们表明，通过引入一种社会规范模型（我们将其视为分散的社会制裁的新兴模式），自利个人群体有可能学习涉及所有关键角色的生产性劳动分工。这种社会规范的作用是在人群中重新分配奖励，抑制反社会角色，同时激励那些本质上报酬不如其他角色的亲社会角色。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了在自利个体中通过去中心化社会规范如何形成劳动分工的机制。
AI.motivation: 人类社会的成功依赖于合作群体中的劳动分工，但自利个体倾向于回避低回报的关键角色，导致社会困境。
AI.method: 构建社会规范模型，通过再分配奖励机制抑制反社会行为并激励关键角色承担。

search_text: The emergence of division of labor through decentralized social sanctioning 通过分散的社会制裁出现劳动分工 Human ecological success relies on our characteristic ability to flexibly self-organize into cooperative social groups, the most successful of which employ substantial specialization and division of labor. Unlike most other animals, humans learn by trial and error during their lives what role to take on. However, when some critical roles are more attractive than others, and individuals are self-interested, then there is a social dilemma: each individual would prefer others take on the critical but unremunerative roles so they may remain free to take one that pays better. But disaster occurs if all act thusly and a critical role goes unfilled. In such situations learning an optimum role distribution may not be possible. Consequently, a fundamental question is: how can division of labor emerge in groups of self-interested lifetime-learning individuals? Here we show that by introducing a model of social norms, which we regard as emergent patterns of decentralized social sanctioning, it becomes possible for groups of self-interested individuals to learn a productive division of labor involving all critical roles. Such social norms work by redistributing rewards within the population to disincentivize antisocial roles while incentivizing prosocial roles that do not intrinsically pay as well as others. 人类生态的成功依赖于我们灵活地自组织成合作性社会群体的特有能力，其中最成功的群体采用了大量的专业化和劳动分工。与大多数其他动物不同，人类在一生中通过尝试和错误来学习扮演什么角色。然而，当某些关键角色比其他角色更有吸引力，并且个人是自私的，那么就会出现一种社会困境：每个人都更喜欢其他人担任关键但无报酬的角色，这样他们就可以自由地担任报酬更高的角色。但如果所有人都这样做并且关键角色没有得到填补，灾难就会发生。在这种情况下，学习最佳的角色分配可能是不可能的。因此，一个基本问题是：如何在自私的终身学习个体群体中出现分工？在这里，我们表明，通过引入一种社会规范模型（我们将其视为分散的社会制裁的新兴模式），自利个人群体有可能学习涉及所有关键角色的生产性劳动分工。这种社会规范的作用是在人群中重新分配奖励，抑制反社会角色，同时激励那些本质上报酬不如其他角色的亲社会角色。 本文探讨了在自利个体中通过去中心化社会规范如何形成劳动分工的机制。 Anil Yaman,Joel Z. Leibo,Giovanni Iacca,Sang Wan Lee cs.MA,cs.AI,cs.NE

authors_text: Amr Gomaa,Alexandra Alles,Elena Meiser,Lydia Helene Rupp,Marco Molz,Guillermo Reyes
title: What's on your mind? A Mental and Perceptual Load Estimation Framework towards Adaptive In-vehicle Interaction while Driving
title_zh: 你在想什么？驾驶时自适应车内交互的心理和感知负荷估计框架
comment: Accepted at AutoUI'22
comments: Accepted at AutoUI'22
abstract: Several researchers have focused on studying driver cognitive behavior and mental load for in-vehicle interaction while driving. Adaptive interfaces that vary with mental and perceptual load levels could help in reducing accidents and enhancing the driver experience. In this paper, we analyze the effects of mental workload and perceptual load on psychophysiological dimensions and provide a machine learning-based framework for mental and perceptual load estimation in a dual task scenario for in-vehicle interaction (https://github.com/amrgomaaelhady/MWL-PL-estimator). We use off-the-shelf non-intrusive sensors that can be easily integrated into the vehicle's system. Our statistical analysis shows that while mental workload influences some psychophysiological dimensions, perceptual load shows little effect. Furthermore, we classify the mental and perceptual load levels through the fusion of these measurements, moving towards a real-time adaptive in-vehicle interface that is personalized to user behavior and driving conditions. We report up to 89% mental workload classification accuracy and provide a real-time minimally-intrusive solution.
summary: Several researchers have focused on studying driver cognitive behavior and mental load for in-vehicle interaction while driving. Adaptive interfaces that vary with mental and perceptual load levels could help in reducing accidents and enhancing the driver experience. In this paper, we analyze the effects of mental workload and perceptual load on psychophysiological dimensions and provide a machine learning-based framework for mental and perceptual load estimation in a dual task scenario for in-vehicle interaction (https://github.com/amrgomaaelhady/MWL-PL-estimator). We use off-the-shelf non-intrusive sensors that can be easily integrated into the vehicle's system. Our statistical analysis shows that while mental workload influences some psychophysiological dimensions, perceptual load shows little effect. Furthermore, we classify the mental and perceptual load levels through the fusion of these measurements, moving towards a real-time adaptive in-vehicle interface that is personalized to user behavior and driving conditions. We report up to 89% mental workload classification accuracy and provide a real-time minimally-intrusive solution.
abstract_zh: 一些研究人员专注于研究驾驶员驾驶时车内交互的认知行为和心理负荷。随心理和感知负荷水平变化的自适应界面有助于减少事故并增强驾驶员体验。在本文中，我们分析了心理负荷和感知负荷对心理生理维度的影响，并提供了一个基于机器学习的框架，用于车内交互的双重任务场景中的心理和感知负荷估计（https://github.com/amrgomaaelhady/MWL-PL-estimator）。我们使用现成的非侵入式传感器，可以轻松集成到车辆系统中。我们的统计分析表明，虽然精神负荷会影响某些心理生理维度，但知觉负荷几乎没有影响。此外，我们通过融合这些测量结果对心理和感知负荷水平进行分类，朝着根据用户行为和驾驶条件个性化的实时自适应车载界面发展。我们报告脑力负荷分类准确率高达 89%，并提供实时、侵入性最小的解决方案。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于机器学习的框架，用于估计驾驶过程中的心理和感知负荷，以实现自适应车载交互系统。
AI.motivation: 通过实时调整车载交互界面来降低事故风险并提升驾驶体验，需要准确评估驾驶员的心理和感知负荷水平。
AI.method: 利用非侵入式传感器采集生理信号，结合统计分析与机器学习方法，融合多维度数据实现负荷分类。
AI.result: 实现了高达89%的心理负荷分类准确率，并提供了实时且最小侵入性的解决方案。
AI.conclusion: 该框架能够根据用户行为和驾驶条件个性化调整界面，为未来智能车载系统提供了可行的技术路径。

authors_text: Jiahao Deng,Eli T. Brown
title: SSDBCODI: Semi-Supervised Density-Based Clustering with Outliers Detection Integrated
title_zh: SSDBCODI：集成了异常值检测的基于半监督密度的聚类
abstract: Clustering analysis is one of the critical tasks in machine learning. Traditionally, clustering has been an independent task, separate from outlier detection. Due to the fact that the performance of clustering can be significantly eroded by outliers, a small number of algorithms try to incorporate outlier detection in the process of clustering. However, most of those algorithms are based on unsupervised partition-based algorithms such as k-means. Given the nature of those algorithms, they often fail to deal with clusters of complex, non-convex shapes. To tackle this challenge, we have proposed SSDBCODI, a semi-supervised density-based algorithm. SSDBCODI combines the advantage of density-based algorithms, which are capable of dealing with clusters of complex shapes, with the semi-supervised element, which offers flexibility to adjust the clustering results based on a few user labels. We also merge an outlier detection component with the clustering process. Potential outliers are detected based on three scores generated during the process: (1) reachability-score, which measures how density-reachable a point is to a labeled normal object, (2) local-density-score, which measures the neighboring density of data objects, and (3) similarity-score, which measures the closeness of a point to its nearest labeled outliers. Then in the following step, instance weights are generated for each data instance based on those three scores before being used to train a classifier for further clustering and outlier detection. To enhance the understanding of the proposed algorithm, for our evaluation, we have run our proposed algorithm against some of the state-of-art approaches on multiple datasets and separately listed the results of outlier detection apart from clustering. Our results indicate that our algorithm can achieve superior results with a small percentage of labels.
summary: Clustering analysis is one of the critical tasks in machine learning. Traditionally, clustering has been an independent task, separate from outlier detection. Due to the fact that the performance of clustering can be significantly eroded by outliers, a small number of algorithms try to incorporate outlier detection in the process of clustering. However, most of those algorithms are based on unsupervised partition-based algorithms such as k-means. Given the nature of those algorithms, they often fail to deal with clusters of complex, non-convex shapes. To tackle this challenge, we have proposed SSDBCODI, a semi-supervised density-based algorithm. SSDBCODI combines the advantage of density-based algorithms, which are capable of dealing with clusters of complex shapes, with the semi-supervised element, which offers flexibility to adjust the clustering results based on a few user labels. We also merge an outlier detection component with the clustering process. Potential outliers are detected based on three scores generated during the process: (1) reachability-score, which measures how density-reachable a point is to a labeled normal object, (2) local-density-score, which measures the neighboring density of data objects, and (3) similarity-score, which measures the closeness of a point to its nearest labeled outliers. Then in the following step, instance weights are generated for each data instance based on those three scores before being used to train a classifier for further clustering and outlier detection. To enhance the understanding of the proposed algorithm, for our evaluation, we have run our proposed algorithm against some of the state-of-art approaches on multiple datasets and separately listed the results of outlier detection apart from clustering. Our results indicate that our algorithm can achieve superior results with a small percentage of labels.
abstract_zh: 聚类分析是机器学习中的关键任务之一。传统上，聚类是一项独立的任务，与异常值检测分开。由于异常值会严重损害聚类的性能，因此少数算法尝试在聚类过程中加入异常值检测。然而，大多数这些算法都是基于无监督的基于分区的算法，例如 k 均值。鉴于这些算法的性质，它们通常无法处理复杂的非凸形状的簇。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了 SSDBCODI，一种基于密度的半监督算法。 SSDBCODI 结合了基于密度的算法的优势（能够处理复杂形状的聚类）和半监督元素（可以根据一些用户标签灵活地调整聚类结果）。我们还将异常值检测组件与聚类过程合并。根据在此过程中生成的三个分数来检测潜在的异常值：（1）可达性分数，用于测量点到标记的正常对象的密度可达性；（2）局部密度分数，用于测量数据对象的邻近密度；（3）相似性分数，用于测量点与其最近的标记异常值的接近程度。然后，在接下来的步骤中，基于这三个分数为每个数据实例生成实例权重，然后用于训练分类器以进行进一步的聚类和异常值检测。为了增强对所提出算法的理解，为了进行评估，我们在多个数据集上针对一些最先进的方法运行了我们所提出的算法，并分别列出了除聚类之外的异常值检测结果。我们的结果表明，我们的算法可以使用少量标签获得优异的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种半监督密度聚类算法SSDBCODI，集成异常检测功能以提升复杂形状聚类效果。
AI.motivation: 传统聚类与异常检测分离导致性能下降，现有算法多基于k-means难以处理非凸聚类，需结合半监督机制与密度方法改进。
AI.method: 融合密度聚类与半监督学习，通过可达性分数、局部密度分数和相似性分数生成实例权重，训练分类器实现聚类与异常检测。
AI.result: 在多数据集测试中，SSDBCODI在少量标签情况下优于现有方法，聚类与异常检测效果均显著提升。
AI.conclusion: SSDBCODI有效整合半监督机制、密度聚类与异常检测，为复杂数据场景提供更优解决方案。

search_text: SSDBCODI: Semi-Supervised Density-Based Clustering with Outliers Detection Integrated SSDBCODI：集成了异常值检测的基于半监督密度的聚类 Clustering analysis is one of the critical tasks in machine learning. Traditionally, clustering has been an independent task, separate from outlier detection. Due to the fact that the performance of clustering can be significantly eroded by outliers, a small number of algorithms try to incorporate outlier detection in the process of clustering. However, most of those algorithms are based on unsupervised partition-based algorithms such as k-means. Given the nature of those algorithms, they often fail to deal with clusters of complex, non-convex shapes. To tackle this challenge, we have proposed SSDBCODI, a semi-supervised density-based algorithm. SSDBCODI combines the advantage of density-based algorithms, which are capable of dealing with clusters of complex shapes, with the semi-supervised element, which offers flexibility to adjust the clustering results based on a few user labels. We also merge an outlier detection component with the clustering process. Potential outliers are detected based on three scores generated during the process: (1) reachability-score, which measures how density-reachable a point is to a labeled normal object, (2) local-density-score, which measures the neighboring density of data objects, and (3) similarity-score, which measures the closeness of a point to its nearest labeled outliers. Then in the following step, instance weights are generated for each data instance based on those three scores before being used to train a classifier for further clustering and outlier detection. To enhance the understanding of the proposed algorithm, for our evaluation, we have run our proposed algorithm against some of the state-of-art approaches on multiple datasets and separately listed the results of outlier detection apart from clustering. Our results indicate that our algorithm can achieve superior results with a small percentage of labels. 聚类分析是机器学习中的关键任务之一。传统上，聚类是一项独立的任务，与异常值检测分开。由于异常值会严重损害聚类的性能，因此少数算法尝试在聚类过程中加入异常值检测。然而，大多数这些算法都是基于无监督的基于分区的算法，例如 k 均值。鉴于这些算法的性质，它们通常无法处理复杂的非凸形状的簇。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了 SSDBCODI，一种基于密度的半监督算法。 SSDBCODI 结合了基于密度的算法的优势（能够处理复杂形状的聚类）和半监督元素（可以根据一些用户标签灵活地调整聚类结果）。我们还将异常值检测组件与聚类过程合并。根据在此过程中生成的三个分数来检测潜在的异常值：（1）可达性分数，用于测量点到标记的正常对象的密度可达性；（2）局部密度分数，用于测量数据对象的邻近密度；（3）相似性分数，用于测量点与其最近的标记异常值的接近程度。然后，在接下来的步骤中，基于这三个分数为每个数据实例生成实例权重，然后用于训练分类器以进行进一步的聚类和异常值检测。为了增强对所提出算法的理解，为了进行评估，我们在多个数据集上针对一些最先进的方法运行了我们所提出的算法，并分别列出了除聚类之外的异常值检测结果。我们的结果表明，我们的算法可以使用少量标签获得优异的结果。 本文提出一种半监督密度聚类算法SSDBCODI，集成异常检测功能以提升复杂形状聚类效果。 Jiahao Deng,Eli T. Brown cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Aditya Aggarwal,Siddhartha Gairola,Uddeshya Upadhyay,Akshay P Vasishta,Diwakar Rao,Aditya Goyal,Kaushik Murali,Nipun Kwatra,Mohit Jain
title: Towards Automating Retinoscopy for Refractive Error Diagnosis
title_zh: 实现屈光不正诊断自动化检影
comment: This paper is accepted for publication in IMWUT 2022
comments: This paper is accepted for publication in IMWUT 2022
abstract: Refractive error is the most common eye disorder and is the key cause behind correctable visual impairment, responsible for nearly 80% of the visual impairment in the US. Refractive error can be diagnosed using multiple methods, including subjective refraction, retinoscopy, and autorefractors. Although subjective refraction is the gold standard, it requires cooperation from the patient and hence is not suitable for infants, young children, and developmentally delayed adults. Retinoscopy is an objective refraction method that does not require any input from the patient. However, retinoscopy requires a lens kit and a trained examiner, which limits its use for mass screening. In this work, we automate retinoscopy by attaching a smartphone to a retinoscope and recording retinoscopic videos with the patient wearing a custom pair of paper frames. We develop a video processing pipeline that takes retinoscopic videos as input and estimates the net refractive error based on our proposed extension of the retinoscopy mathematical model. Our system alleviates the need for a lens kit and can be performed by an untrained examiner. In a clinical trial with 185 eyes, we achieved a sensitivity of 91.0% and specificity of 74.0% on refractive error diagnosis. Moreover, the mean absolute error of our approach was 0.75$\pm$0.67D on net refractive error estimation compared to subjective refraction measurements. Our results indicate that our approach has the potential to be used as a retinoscopy-based refractive error screening tool in real-world medical settings.

abstract_zh: 屈光不正是最常见的眼部疾病，也是可矫正视力障碍的关键原因，导致美国近 80% 的视力障碍。屈光不正可以通过多种方法来诊断，包括主观验光、检影和自动验光仪。虽然主观验光是金标准，但它需要患者的配合，因此不适合婴儿、幼儿和发育迟缓的成年人。检影验光是一种客观的验光方法，不需要患者任何输入。然而，检影检查需要镜片套件和训练有素的检查员，这限制了其在大规模筛查中的使用。在这项工作中，我们通过将智能手机连接到检影镜并在患者佩戴一对定制纸框的情况下录制检影视频来自动化检影检查。我们开发了一种视频处理管道，以检影视频作为输入，并根据我们提出的检影数学模型的扩展来估计净屈光不正。我们的系统减少了对镜头套件的需求，并且可以由未经培训的检查员执行。在一项涉及 185 只眼睛的临床试验中，我们对屈光不正诊断的敏感性为 91.0%，特异性为 74.0%。此外，与主观验光测量相比，我们方法的净屈光不正估计的平均绝对误差为 0.75$\pm$0.67D。我们的结果表明，我们的方法有潜力在现实世界的医疗环境中用作基于检影检查的屈光不正筛查工具。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于智能手机自动化的检影法系统，用于诊断屈光不正。
AI.motivation: 传统检影法需要专业设备和训练有素的检查者，限制了其在大规模筛查中的应用，而主观验光则依赖患者配合，不适用于儿童或特殊人群。
AI.method: 将智能手机连接至检影镜，使用定制纸框记录检影视频，并开发视频处理流程，基于改进的检影数学模型估算屈光不正度数。
AI.result: 临床试验显示，系统对屈光不正诊断的灵敏度为91.0%，特异度为74.0%，与主观验光相比，平均绝对误差为0.75±0.67D。
AI.conclusion: 该方法可作为无需专业设备和训练的屈光不正筛查工具，具有实际医疗应用潜力。

authors_text: Ahura Jami,Mahdi Razzaghpour,Hussein Alnuweiri,Yaser P. Fallah
title: Augmented Driver Behavior Models for High-Fidelity Simulation Study of Crash Detection Algorithms
title_zh: 用于碰撞检测算法高保真仿真研究的增强驾驶员行为模型
abstract: Developing safety and efficiency applications for Connected and Automated Vehicles (CAVs) require a great deal of testing and evaluation. The need for the operation of these systems in critical and dangerous situations makes the burden of their evaluation very costly, possibly dangerous, and time-consuming. As an alternative, researchers attempt to study and evaluate their algorithms and designs using simulation platforms. Modeling the behavior of drivers or human operators in CAVs or other vehicles interacting with them is one of the main challenges of such simulations. While developing a perfect model for human behavior is a challenging task and an open problem, we present a significant augmentation of the current models used in simulators for driver behavior. In this paper, we present a simulation platform for a hybrid transportation system that includes both human-driven and automated vehicles. In addition, we decompose the human driving task and offer a modular approach to simulating a large-scale traffic scenario, allowing for a thorough investigation of automated and active safety systems. Such representation through Interconnected modules offers a human-interpretable system that can be tuned to represent different classes of drivers. Additionally, we analyze a large driving dataset to extract expressive parameters that would best describe different driving characteristics. Finally, we recreate a similarly dense traffic scenario within our simulator and conduct a thorough analysis of various human-specific and system-specific factors, studying their effect on traffic network performance and safety.
summary: Developing safety and efficiency applications for Connected and Automated Vehicles (CAVs) require a great deal of testing and evaluation. The need for the operation of these systems in critical and dangerous situations makes the burden of their evaluation very costly, possibly dangerous, and time-consuming. As an alternative, researchers attempt to study and evaluate their algorithms and designs using simulation platforms. Modeling the behavior of drivers or human operators in CAVs or other vehicles interacting with them is one of the main challenges of such simulations. While developing a perfect model for human behavior is a challenging task and an open problem, we present a significant augmentation of the current models used in simulators for driver behavior. In this paper, we present a simulation platform for a hybrid transportation system that includes both human-driven and automated vehicles. In addition, we decompose the human driving task and offer a modular approach to simulating a large-scale traffic scenario, allowing for a thorough investigation of automated and active safety systems. Such representation through Interconnected modules offers a human-interpretable system that can be tuned to represent different classes of drivers. Additionally, we analyze a large driving dataset to extract expressive parameters that would best describe different driving characteristics. Finally, we recreate a similarly dense traffic scenario within our simulator and conduct a thorough analysis of various human-specific and system-specific factors, studying their effect on traffic network performance and safety.
abstract_zh: 为联网和自动驾驶车辆 (CAV) 开发安全和高效的应用程序需要大量的测试和评估。这些系统需要在危急和危险的情况下运行，这使得评估它们的负担非常昂贵，可能很危险并且耗时。作为替代方案，研究人员尝试使用模拟平台来研究和评估他们的算法和设计。对 CAV 或其他与其交互的车辆中的驾驶员或操作人员的行为进行建模是此类模拟的主要挑战之一。虽然开发人类行为的完美模型是一项具有挑战性的任务和一个开放的问题，但我们提出了对驾驶员行为模拟器中当前使用的模型的显着增强。在本文中，我们提出了一个混合交通系统的仿真平台，其中包括人力驾驶和自动驾驶车辆。此外，我们分解了人类驾驶任务，并提供了一种模块化方法来模拟大规模交通场景，从而可以对自动化和主动安全系统进行彻底研究。通过互连模块的这种表示提供了人类可解释的系统，可以调整该系统来表示不同类别的驾驶员。此外，我们分析大型驾驶数据集，以提取最能描述不同驾驶特征的表达参数。最后，我们在模拟器中重新创建了一个类似的密集交通场景，并对各种特定于人类和特定于系统的因素进行了彻底分析，研究它们对交通网络性能和安全性的影响。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种增强的驾驶员行为模型，用于构建混合交通系统的高保真仿真平台，以研究碰撞检测算法。
AI.motivation: 联网自动驾驶车辆（CAVs）的安全性与效率应用需要大量测试，但真实场景测试成本高且危险，需通过仿真平台替代。
AI.method: 构建包含人工驾驶与自动驾驶车辆的混合系统，分解驾驶任务并采用模块化仿真方法，结合真实驾驶数据提取特征参数进行场景重建。
AI.result: 分析了人类与系统特异性因素对交通网络性能与安全的影响，验证了模型在复杂交通场景中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为自动化安全系统提供了可解释且可调节的仿真框架，支持大规模交通场景的深入研究。

While developing a perfect model for human behavior is a challenging task and an open problem, we present a significant augmentation of the current models used in simulators for driver behavior. In this paper, we present a simulation platform for a hybrid transportation system that includes both human-driven and automated vehicles. In addition, we decompose the human driving task and offer a modular approach to simulating a large-scale traffic scenario, allowing for a thorough investigation of automated and active safety systems. Such representation through Interconnected modules offers a human-interpretable system that can be tuned to represent different classes of drivers. Additionally, we analyze a large driving dataset to extract expressive parameters that would best describe different driving characteristics. Finally, we recreate a similarly dense traffic scenario within our simulator and conduct a thorough analysis of various human-specific and system-specific factors, studying their effect on traffic network performance and safety. 为联网和自动驾驶车辆 (CAV) 开发安全和高效的应用程序需要大量的测试和评估。这些系统需要在危急和危险的情况下运行，这使得评估它们的负担非常昂贵，可能很危险并且耗时。作为替代方案，研究人员尝试使用模拟平台来研究和评估他们的算法和设计。对 CAV 或其他与其交互的车辆中的驾驶员或操作人员的行为进行建模是此类模拟的主要挑战之一。虽然开发人类行为的完美模型是一项具有挑战性的任务和一个开放的问题，但我们提出了对驾驶员行为模拟器中当前使用的模型的显着增强。在本文中，我们提出了一个混合交通系统的仿真平台，其中包括人力驾驶和自动驾驶车辆。此外，我们分解了人类驾驶任务，并提供了一种模块化方法来模拟大规模交通场景，从而可以对自动化和主动安全系统进行彻底研究。通过互连模块的这种表示提供了人类可解释的系统，可以调整该系统来表示不同类别的驾驶员。此外，我们分析大型驾驶数据集，以提取最能描述不同驾驶特征的表达参数。最后，我们在模拟器中重新创建了一个类似的密集交通场景，并对各种特定于人类和特定于系统的因素进行了彻底分析，研究它们对交通网络性能和安全性的影响。 本文提出一种增强的驾驶员行为模型，用于构建混合交通系统的高保真仿真平台，以研究碰撞检测算法。 Ahura Jami,Mahdi Razzaghpour,Hussein Alnuweiri,Yaser P. Fallah cs.MA,cs.AI,cs.RO

authors_text: Ammar Alsheghri,Farnoosh Ghadiri,Ying Zhang,Olivier Lessard,Julia Keren,Farida Cheriet,Francois Guibault
title: Semi-supervised segmentation of tooth from 3D Scanned Dental Arches
title_zh: 根据 3D 扫描牙弓对牙齿进行半监督分割
comment: ef:In Medical Imaging 2022: Image Processing (Vol. 12032, pp. 766-771). SPIE 2022
comments: ef:In Medical Imaging 2022: Image Processing (Vol. 12032, pp. 766-771). SPIE 2022
abstract: Teeth segmentation is an important topic in dental restorations that is essential for crown generation, diagnosis, and treatment planning. In the dental field, the variability of input data is high and there are no publicly available 3D dental arch datasets. Although there has been improvement in the field provided by recent deep learning architectures on 3D data, there still exists some problems such as properly identifying missing teeth in an arch. We propose to use spectral clustering as a self-supervisory signal to joint-train neural networks for segmentation of 3D arches. Our approach is motivated by the observation that K-means clustering provides cues to capture margin lines related to human perception. The main idea is to automatically generate training data by decomposing unlabeled 3D arches into segments relying solely on geometric information. The network is then trained using a joint loss that combines a supervised loss of annotated input and a self-supervised loss of non-labeled input. Our collected data has a variety of arches including arches with missing teeth. Our experimental results show improvement over the fully supervised state-of-the-art MeshSegNet when using semi-supervised learning. Finally, we contribute code and a dataset.
summary: Teeth segmentation is an important topic in dental restorations that is essential for crown generation, diagnosis, and treatment planning. In the dental field, the variability of input data is high and there are no publicly available 3D dental arch datasets. Although there has been improvement in the field provided by recent deep learning architectures on 3D data, there still exists some problems such as properly identifying missing teeth in an arch. We propose to use spectral clustering as a self-supervisory signal to joint-train neural networks for segmentation of 3D arches. Our approach is motivated by the observation that K-means clustering provides cues to capture margin lines related to human perception. The main idea is to automatically generate training data by decomposing unlabeled 3D arches into segments relying solely on geometric information. The network is then trained using a joint loss that combines a supervised loss of annotated input and a self-supervised loss of non-labeled input. Our collected data has a variety of arches including arches with missing teeth. Our experimental results show improvement over the fully supervised state-of-the-art MeshSegNet when using semi-supervised learning. Finally, we contribute code and a dataset.
abstract_zh: 牙齿分割是牙科修复中的一个重要主题，对于牙冠生成、诊断和治疗计划至关重要。在牙科领域，输入数据的可变性很高，并且没有公开可用的 3D 牙弓数据集。尽管最近的 3D 数据深度学习架构在该领域取得了进步，但仍然存在一些问题，例如正确识别牙弓中缺失的牙齿。我们建议使用谱聚类作为自我监督信号来联合训练神经网络来分割 3D 拱门。我们的方法的动机是观察到 K 均值聚类提供了捕获与人类感知相关的边缘线的线索。主要思想是通过将未标记的 3D 拱门分解为仅依赖于几何信息的片段来自动生成训练数据。然后使用联合损失来训练网络，该联合损失结合了带注释输入的监督损失和非标记输入的自监督损失。我们收集的数据有各种牙弓，包括缺牙的牙弓。我们的实验结果表明，使用半监督学习时，比完全监督的最先进的 MeshSegNet 有所改进。最后，我们贡献代码和数据集。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于半监督学习的3D牙弓牙齿分割方法，利用谱聚类生成自监督信号以提升分割效果。
AI.motivation: 传统深度学习方法在处理缺失牙齿和缺乏公开3D牙弓数据集的情况下表现不足，需通过自监督学习解决标注数据稀缺问题。
AI.method: 采用谱聚类分解未标注3D牙弓的几何信息生成伪标签，结合标注数据与非标注数据的联合损失函数进行网络训练。
AI.result: 在半监督学习下，分割性能优于全监督的MeshSegNet方法，并贡献了包含缺失牙齿的牙弓数据集与代码。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过几何引导的自监督学习有效解决了牙齿分割中的缺失牙齿识别问题，为牙科应用提供了实用的解决方案。

authors_text: Lik Hang Lee,Carlos Bermejo Fernandez,Ahmad Alhilal,Tristan Braud,Simo Hosio,Pan Hui,Esmée Henrieke Henrieke Anne de Haas
title: Beyond the Blue Sky of Multimodal Interaction: A Centennial Vision of Interplanetary Virtual Spaces in Turn-based Metaverse
title_zh: 超越多模态交互的蓝天：回合制元宇宙中星际虚拟空间的百年愿景
comment: Accepted Paper in ACM ICMI 2022 (Blue Sky Track)
comments: Accepted Paper in ACM ICMI 2022 (Blue Sky Track)
abstract: Human habitation across multiple planets requires communication and social connection between planets. When the infrastructure of a deep space network becomes mature, immersive cyberspace, known as the Metaverse, can exchange diversified user data and host multitudinous virtual worlds. Nevertheless, such immersive cyberspace unavoidably encounters latency in minutes, and thus operates in a turn-taking manner. This Blue Sky paper illustrates a vision of an interplanetary Metaverse that connects Earthian and Martian users in a turn-based Metaverse. Accordingly, we briefly discuss several grand challenges to catalyze research initiatives for the `Digital Big Bang' on Mars.
summary: Human habitation across multiple planets requires communication and social connection between planets. When the infrastructure of a deep space network becomes mature, immersive cyberspace, known as the Metaverse, can exchange diversified user data and host multitudinous virtual worlds. Nevertheless, such immersive cyberspace unavoidably encounters latency in minutes, and thus operates in a turn-taking manner. This Blue Sky paper illustrates a vision of an interplanetary Metaverse that connects Earthian and Martian users in a turn-based Metaverse. Accordingly, we briefly discuss several grand challenges to catalyze research initiatives for the `Digital Big Bang' on Mars.
abstract_zh: 人类在多个星球上的居住需要星球之间的沟通和社会联系。当深空网络基础设施成熟后，沉浸式网络空间（元宇宙）可以交换多样化的用户数据并承载众多的虚拟世界。然而，这种沉浸式的网络空间不可避免地会遇到几分钟的延迟，从而以轮流的方式运行。这篇蓝天论文阐述了星际虚拟宇宙的愿景，该虚拟宇宙在回合制虚拟宇宙中将地球和火星用户连接起来。因此，我们简要讨论了促进火星“数字大爆炸”研究计划的几个重大挑战。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于轮流机制的星际元宇宙架构，解决多行星间通信延迟问题，促进火星数字发展
AI.motivation: 随着人类向多行星扩展，传统实时元宇宙无法满足深空通信延迟，需探索适应分钟级延迟的轮流机制元宇宙
AI.method: 通过分析延迟问题和现有技术限制，提出基于轮流交互的元宇宙框架，并探讨其技术挑战和解决方案
AI.result: 明确了实现星际元宇宙的关键挑战，为后续研究提供方向，如数据同步、用户体验优化等
AI.conclusion: 基于轮流机制的元宇宙是实现星际社会连接的可行路径，需要跨学科合作推动技术发展

authors_text: Thao Nguyen,Gabriel Ilharco,Mitchell Wortsman,Sewoong Oh,Ludwig Schmidt
title: Quality Not Quantity: On the Interaction between Dataset Design and Robustness of CLIP
title_zh: 质量而非数量：论数据集设计与 CLIP 稳健性之间的相互作用
comment: Oral paper at NeurIPS 2022
comments: Oral paper at NeurIPS 2022
abstract: Web-crawled datasets have enabled remarkable generalization capabilities in recent image-text models such as CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image pre-training) or Flamingo, but little is known about the dataset creation processes. In this work, we introduce a testbed of six publicly available data sources - YFCC, LAION, Conceptual Captions, WIT, RedCaps, Shutterstock - to investigate how pre-training distributions induce robustness in CLIP. We find that the performance of the pre-training data varies substantially across distribution shifts, with no single data source dominating. Moreover, we systematically study the interactions between these data sources and find that combining multiple sources does not necessarily yield better models, but rather dilutes the robustness of the best individual data source. We complement our empirical findings with theoretical insights from a simple setting, where combining the training data also results in diluted robustness. In addition, our theoretical model provides a candidate explanation for the success of the CLIP-based data filtering technique recently employed in the LAION dataset. Overall our results demonstrate that simply gathering a large amount of data from the web is not the most effective way to build a pre-training dataset for robust generalization, necessitating further study into dataset design. Code is available at https://github.com/mlfoundations/clip_quality_not_quantity.
summary: Web-crawled datasets have enabled remarkable generalization capabilities in recent image-text models such as CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image pre-training) or Flamingo, but little is known about the dataset creation processes. In this work, we introduce a testbed of six publicly available data sources - YFCC, LAION, Conceptual Captions, WIT, RedCaps, Shutterstock - to investigate how pre-training distributions induce robustness in CLIP. We find that the performance of the pre-training data varies substantially across distribution shifts, with no single data source dominating. Moreover, we systematically study the interactions between these data sources and find that combining multiple sources does not necessarily yield better models, but rather dilutes the robustness of the best individual data source. We complement our empirical findings with theoretical insights from a simple setting, where combining the training data also results in diluted robustness. In addition, our theoretical model provides a candidate explanation for the success of the CLIP-based data filtering technique recently employed in the LAION dataset. Overall our results demonstrate that simply gathering a large amount of data from the web is not the most effective way to build a pre-training dataset for robust generalization, necessitating further study into dataset design. Code is available at https://github.com/mlfoundations/clip_quality_not_quantity.
abstract_zh: 网络爬取的数据集在最近的图像文本模型中实现了显着的泛化能力，例如 CLIP（对比语言图像预训练）或 Flamingo，但人们对数据集创建过程知之甚少。在这项工作中，我们引入了一个包含六个公开可用数据源（YFCC、LAION、Conceptual Captions、WIT、RedCaps、Shutterstock）的测试平台，以研究预训练分布如何增强 CLIP 的稳健性。我们发现预训练数据的性能随着分布变化而变化很大，没有一个数据源占主导地位。此外，我们系统地研究了这些数据源之间的相互作用，发现组合多个数据源并不一定会产生更好的模型，而是会削弱最佳单个数据源的稳健性。我们通过简单设置中的理论见解来补充我们的实证研究结果，其中结合训练数据也会导致鲁棒性减弱。此外，我们的理论模型为最近在 LAION 数据集中采用的基于 CLIP 的数据过滤技术的成功提供了候选解释。总的来说，我们的结果表明，简单地从网络收集大量数据并不是构建预训练数据集以实现稳健泛化的最有效方法，因此需要进一步研究数据集设计。代码可在 https://github.com/mlfoundations/clip_quality_not_quantity 获取。

AI.tldr: 该论文研究了不同数据集设计对CLIP模型鲁棒性的影响，通过分析六个公开数据源的分布特性及其组合效果。
AI.motivation: 尽管网络爬取的数据集提升了图像-文本模型的泛化能力，但数据集构建过程对模型鲁棒性的影响尚不明确，需深入探究。
AI.method: 构建包含YFCC、LAION等六个数据源的测试集，通过实验分析单源与多源数据对CLIP鲁棒性的影响，并结合理论模型解释现象。
AI.result: 单一数据源性能随分布变化差异显著，多源组合未提升鲁棒性反而可能削弱；理论模型验证了数据混合导致鲁棒性稀释的现象，并解释了LAION过滤技术的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 单纯追求数据量并非构建鲁棒预训练数据集的最佳方式，需重视数据分布设计与组合策略的研究。
